skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5-120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that turns natural language descriptions into short videos via the Pexo platform.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 534 potential issues, predominantly in reference documentation files showing example commands (SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md, SKILL.md). These are documentation commands, not executable injection vectors. The shell scripts (scripts/*.sh) use standard bash patterns: curl for API calls, jq for JSON processing, and mktemp for temporary files. The PEXO_API_KEY is used only for API Bearer token authentication to pexo.ai. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or command injection was found. Risk level is medium because the skill legitimately executes external commands, accesses the network, reads credentials, and writes to the filesystem as part of its core functionality.

medium_findings.title: Shell script execution via command substitution
medium_findings.description: The bash scripts (scripts/*.sh) use shell command substitution ($(...)) to execute curl, jq, and other commands. This is expected behavior for CLI tools interacting with an API. All commands are hardcoded with no dynamic user input injection vectors identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution is genuine bash CLI tool behavior. Commands are hardcoded, not dynamically constructed from untrusted input. No evidence of command injection.

medium_findings.title: API credential usage in environment variables
medium_findings.description: The skill reads PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL from environment variables. The API key is used exclusively as a Bearer token in HTTP Authorization headers for API calls to pexo.ai. No credential exfiltration or unexpected transmission was identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API credentials are required for the skill to function. Usage is limited to legitimate API authentication only. No obfuscation or exfiltration detected.

medium_findings.title: Network API calls to external service
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to pexo.ai for project management, file upload, and video production. All URLs point to the legitimate pexo.ai domain. No connections to unknown or suspicious endpoints were identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network requests go to pexo.ai, the legitimate service this skill wraps. URLs are hardcoded configuration values, not dynamically resolved from untrusted input.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem access for configuration and temp files
medium_findings.description: The skill reads configuration from ~/.pexo/config and creates temporary files using mktemp for API response handling. Temp files are consistently cleaned up with rm -f. No persistent file storage of sensitive data was identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem access is standard for CLI tools: config file reading and temp file management. All temp files are cleaned up. The config file path (~/.pexo/config) is a standard dotfile location.

low_findings.title: Example shell commands in documentation (false positive)
low_findings.description: Reference documentation (SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) contains example shell commands for user instruction. These are not executed code and do not represent an injection vector.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown documentation files containing example commands for user reference. They are not executed by any script or process.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation and SKILL.md
low_findings.description: Several hardcoded URLs (pexo.ai, pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com) appear in documentation and SKILL.md. These are legitimate service endpoints for the Pexo video platform.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to pexo.ai domains. These are the legitimate API endpoints for the Pexo video production platform. No unknown or suspicious domains detected.

low_findings.title: Heuristic warning: dangerous combination of risk factors
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged the combination of external_commands + network + credential access as suspicious. In context, this is expected behavior for an API-authenticated CLI tool: it executes shell commands (curl/jq) to make authenticated API requests to a legitimate service. No exfiltration or malicious patterns identified.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The combination of code execution, network access, and credential usage is the expected pattern for any CLI tool that authenticates to an API. However, the density of signals warrants documentation as a low-level finding for transparency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution with curl for API calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts use curl via command substitution ($(...)) to make HTTP requests. This is a legitimate pattern for bash-based API clients. All curl commands use hardcoded URLs, headers, and timeout values. No user input flows into command construction.

dangerous_patterns.title: API key usage as Bearer token in HTTP headers
dangerous_patterns.description: The PEXO_API_KEY environment variable is read and passed as an Authorization: Bearer header in API requests. This is the standard authentication method for REST APIs. The key is read from environment variables or a config file, not hardcoded.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configuration file reading from ~/.pexo/config
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts source ~/.pexo/config which contains API credentials. This is a standard configuration pattern for CLI tools. The file path is customizable via the PEXO_CONFIG environment variable.

content.user_title: Produce short AI videos with the Pexo agent
content.value_statement: Creating professional short videos with AI requires complex tooling and prompt engineering expertise. Pexo Agent bridges the gap between your natural language description and a production-ready video, handling scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music selection automatically.
actual_capabilities: Create video projects by describing your idea in natural language
Upload images, videos, and audio files to use as reference in your video
Send messages to the Pexo AI and receive video preview options
Poll for video rendering progress and receive final download links
Request revisions to existing videos using the same project
Check account credit balance and run environment diagnostics
limitations: Video length is limited to 5-120 seconds depending on your plan
Rendering takes approximately 15-20 minutes for a short video
A valid Pexo API key and internet connection are required
Cannot create videos from web URLs directly without downloading files first

use_cases.title: Product marketing videos
use_cases.description: Describe a product and upload reference images to generate a short promotional video for social media or your website.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and small business owners

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Create TikTok, Instagram Reels, or YouTube Shorts by describing your content idea in natural language without video editing skills.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Brand explainer videos
use_cases.description: Produce a short explainer or company introduction video by telling the AI what your brand does and who it serves.
use_cases.target_user: Founders, startup teams, and communication leads

prompt_templates.title: Create a new video project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product video for my new eco-friendly water bottle. Make it bright and modern.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Create a video with uploaded images
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 30-second Instagram Reel showcasing our summer collection. I have uploaded three product photos to use.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to include their own images as reference in the video

prompt_templates.title: Request a revision to an existing video
prompt_templates.prompt: The video is great but please make the开场 more energetic and use faster transitions. Keep the music the same.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has received a video and wants to refine it

prompt_templates.title: Multi-asset video with audio track
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 60-second brand story video. I am uploading 5 product images and a background music track. Use the music as the main audio.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating a complex video with multiple reference files

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second product video for my new ceramic coffee mug.
output: Your video request has been sent to Pexo.
Video delivered with download link and project page URL.

output_examples.input: Make an Instagram Story about our weekend sale. Here are two product photos to use.
output: Request submitted to Pexo for processing.
Final video ready with download link.

best_practices: Pass the user's request to Pexo exactly as written. Adding your own creative direction reduces video quality.
Wait at least 60 seconds between status polls. Calling pexo-chat.sh during processing triggers duplicate production.
Download files from web URLs before uploading them to Pexo. Pexo cannot crawl web URLs directly.
anti_patterns: Adding your own creative descriptions or video parameters that the user did not specify. This overrides Pexo's professional judgment.
Calling pexo-chat.sh while the project status is still WAIT. This triggers duplicate video production and wastes credits.
Sending truncated asset download URLs. The full URL including all query parameters must be sent to avoid 403 errors.

faq.question: What video lengths does Pexo support?
faq.answer: Pexo produces videos between 5 and 120 seconds. The supported aspect ratios are 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1.

faq.question: What file types can I upload as reference?
faq.answer: You can upload images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), and audio files (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: A short video takes approximately 15-20 minutes to produce. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a Pexo API key and a Pexo base URL. Set them as environment variables or in the ~/.pexo/config file.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is delivered?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project ID and send your revision request via pexo-chat.sh with the user's exact feedback.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are available for videos?
faq.answer: Pexo supports three aspect ratios: 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait for TikTok/Reels), and 1:1 (square).
